entries.title: Frodo Is Wounded At Weathertop

context.label: Frodo wounded by the Morgul blade

content.fact: At Weathertop, Frodo is wounded by a Morgul blade, creating an urgent spiritual and physical threat that requires Elvish aid in Rivendell.
content.injection: After Weathertop and before Rivendell healing, Frodo should be weak, endangered, and under a wound that is more than ordinary injury.
constraints: The wound can create coldness, fading, fear, or spiritual peril.
Aragorn can treat and delay the danger but not fully resolve it.
antiLore: Do not treat the wound as a simple cut healed by rest.
Do not make Frodo fully recovered before Rivendell.

entries.title: Glorfindel Has The Book Rescue Role

context.label: Glorfindel Has The Book Rescue Role

content.fact: In book continuity, Glorfindel helps bring Frodo toward Rivendell during the Flight to the Ford.
content.injection: In book continuity, Glorfindel is the major Elven rescuer on the road to Rivendell. Do not replace this with film-only Arwen rescue beats unless an AU or film-continuity overlay is active.
constraints: This is an adaptation guard for book-canon scenes.
Arwen should not be inserted into the Ford rescue by default in this deck.
antiLore: Do not silently import the film Flight to the Ford sequence.

entries.title: Boromir Is Tempted By The Ring

context.label: Boromir under Ring temptation

content.fact: Boromir is drawn toward the idea of using the Ring for Gondor, mixing patriotic desperation with the Ring's corrupting temptation.
content.injection: When Boromir and the Ring are relevant, portray his temptation as rooted in Gondor's desperation and his desire to defend his people, not simple villainy.
constraints: His pressure should escalate toward Amon Hen.
The temptation can coexist with courage and honor.
antiLore: Do not make Boromir evil from the start.
Do not make his desire for the Ring purely selfish or detached from Gondor.

coordinates.label: Company of the Ring
Book II: The Ring Goes South

entries.title: Aragorn Takes Leadership After Gandalf Falls

context.label: Aragorn leads the Fellowship after Moria

content.fact: After Gandalf falls in Moria, Aragorn becomes the Fellowship's practical leader while carrying grief and the burden of uncertain decisions.
content.injection: After Moria, portray Aragorn as assuming hard practical leadership, not as a triumphant king. His authority is necessary but emotionally burdened.
constraints: Boromir may still challenge or pressure decisions.
Aragorn's leadership is tested by loss and by the Fellowship's fragility.
antiLore: Do not leave the Fellowship leaderless in every practical sense after Moria.
Do not make Aragorn's leadership feel politically settled as kingship yet.

coordinates.label: Company of the Ring

entries.title: Gandalf Falls In Moria

context.label: After Gandalf falls in Moria

content.fact: At the Bridge of Khazad-dum, Gandalf falls while confronting Durin's Bane, leaving the Fellowship shocked and leaderless.
content.injection: After Gandalf falls in Moria, the Fellowship should carry grief, shock, and a leadership vacuum. Do not reveal his return until a later, properly gated deck context.
constraints: Aragorn must assume practical leadership after the fall.
The loss should affect morale and decision-making.
antiLore: Do not have characters know Gandalf will return during the Fellowship deck.
Do not treat Gandalf's fall as a trivial inconvenience.

coordinates.label: Company of the Ring
Book II: The Ring Goes South

entries.title: Galadriel Refuses The Ring

context.label: Galadriel tested by the Ring

content.fact: Galadriel is tempted by the One Ring but refuses it, recognizing that accepting it would transform her power into domination.
content.injection: When Frodo offers the Ring to Galadriel, portray the temptation as real and terrible, and her refusal as a decisive rejection of domination.
constraints: The refusal does not mean the Ring lacked power over her; it means she passed the test.
Her response should reinforce that the Ring cannot be used safely for good.
antiLore: Do not make Galadriel casually immune to the Ring.
Do not let her take and safely wield the Ring.

coordinates.label: Company of the Ring

entries.title: Merry And Pippin Are Taken By Orcs

context.label: Merry and Pippin captured at the breaking

content.fact: During the breaking of the Fellowship, Merry and Pippin are taken by Orcs, creating the pursuit path for Aragorn, Legolas, and Gimli.
content.injection: After the breaking, Merry and Pippin should not remain free with Aragorn. Their capture motivates the Three Hunters to pursue the Orcs.
constraints: Boromir's final defense of them matters if the scene includes that moment.
Their capture transitions into Rohan and Fangorn storylines.
antiLore: Do not have Merry and Pippin continue with Frodo and Sam.
Do not skip the pursuit consequence for Aragorn, Legolas, and Gimli.
